rulings.Case Title: STARKDRIVE LLC VS. CHRISTIAN ADEL MICHAEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default Judgment **First Appearance Fees Paid**
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 2, 2025, line 12, DEFENDANT CHRISTIAN MICHAEL'S Motion For Relief From Default Judgment Christian Adel Michael's motion for relief from default judgment is granted. The court grants Michael's request for judicial notice of documents in the federal action. The court denies his request to strike the late-filed opposition brief and exercises its discretion to consider the opposing and reply briefs *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ASMAHAN ATTAYEB VS. MATTHEW C. MULLENWEG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-22-600095 WITH CASE CGC-22-600093
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 2, 2025, line 2, PLAINTIFF ASMAHAN ATTAYEB'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-22-600095 WITH CASE CGC-22-600093 The motion by plaintiffs to consolidate Case No. CGC-22-600093 (Attayeb matter) with Case No. CGC-22-600095 (Westmoreland matter) is denied. This motion was previously decided by the court on December 17, 2024. Plaintiffs present no new material facts or law (CCP 1008(b)) but rather contend that the motion was denied "due to the confusion regarding the notice" of motion and for what purposes consolidation was sought. This is not a change in facts or law that would warrant reconsideration of a prior order. The only changed circumstance Plaintiffs cite is that contemplated summary judgment motions were not filed. But the prospect of a confusing record on summary judgment was one of several reasons this court relied on to deny consolidation; this changed circumstance is not a new material fact warranting reconsideration. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DR. DOE VS. KAISER PERMANENTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And Form Interrogatories, Set Three; And For Monetary Sanctions [Ccp 2030.290, 2031.300, 2023.030]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 2, 2025, line 3, DEFENDANTS KASIER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And Form Interrogatories, Set Three; And For Monetary Sanctions [Ccp 2030.290, 2031.300, 2023.030] Defendants Kaiser Foundation Hospitals and the Permanent Medical Group Inc. (collectively Kaiser)'s unopposed motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents, set one, and form interrogatories, set three, and for monetary sanctions, is granted in part and denied in part. Respecting the third set of form interrogatories, plaintiff Dr. Doe has not responded. (Sha Dec. para. 8.) Doe has waived any objections and is ordered to provide complete, objection-free, verified responses by May 21, 2025. Respecting the first set of requests for production, Doe has not yet verified his responses. (Sha Dec. para. 5.) The court orders Doe to verify his responses by May 21, 2025. Kaiser also asks for an order that Doe further amend his amended responses. But Kaiser has not put before the court the responses or a separate statement setting out the discovery for which it is seeking amendment. Kaiser has not carried its burden of showing that Doe's responses are noncompliant. Doe is ordered to produce responsive documents if any are outstanding by May 21, 2025. Kaiser seeks sanctions. The court awards partial sanctions to reflect Kaiser's mixed success with this motion. The court does not find Doe's position to be substantially justified. Doe shall pay $2000 to Kaiser Foundation Hospitals and the Permanente Medical Group by May 21, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: HECTOR CHAVEZ BARRAGAN VS. GENERAL MOTORS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 2, 2025, line 6, 1 - DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication Defendant General Motors LLC's motion for summary judgment is continued on the court's own motion to May 23, 2025. The court finds good cause to continue this matter to a date within 30 days of trial. (See CCP 437c(a)(3).) The continuance will postpone hearing until plaintiff Hector Chavez Barragan has an opportunity to seek a continuance of the trial date in this matter; obtaining a continuance is a condition of the court's companion order granting leave to amend and whether the complaint is amended will determine whether the summary judgment motion is moot. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: BONELLI ENTERPRISES, LLC, VS. TANTALUS BUILDERS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 2, 2025, line 7, CROSS DEFENDANT BONELLI ENTERPRISES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT "[T]he court retains authority to dismiss an action if an unrepresented corporation does not obtain counsel within reasonable time." (CLD Construction, Inc. v. City of San Ramon (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1141, 1150.) Here, Tantalus Builders, Inc., has been on notice of the need to secure counsel since at least February 18, 2025. Trial is set for July 7, 2025. Moving party Bonelli Enterprises should not be required to wait until the eve of trial to learn whether Tantalus has secured representation. The court continues this matter on its own motion to May 13, 2025 to give Tantalus a final opportunity to obtain counsel and anticipates granting the requested relief if that has not occurred. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)
